moggingMatrix.description: Pairwise mogging coefficients for 15 popular bots. μ = ΔPSL × V × 100 where V = (MP/10) × (1 + FP/10)

matrix.analysis: React's component model and hooks ecosystem completely overshadow Express's aging middleware paradigm. The 2.0 PSL gap is amplified by React's massive visibility.
React's modern architecture brutally exposes Webpack's configuration complexity. With a 3.2 PSL gap, React makes Webpack look like technical debt incarnate.
Vite's instant dev server and ESM-first approach demolish Webpack's slow bundling. This is generational replacement in action - the old guard getting completely mogged.
Vitest's modern DX edges out Express's dated patterns. Small PSL gap but sufficient for moderate mogging given Vitest's growing visibility.
React's ecosystem depth and industry adoption brutally mog Vue. While Vue has elegant APIs (HC 8.3), it can't match React's market dominance and feature prominence.
Fastify's modern architecture and performance marginally mog Express. The PSL gap is narrow (0.2), but Fastify's superior scalability potential (8.3 vs 7.3) gives it the edge.
Lodash's functional purity and consistency slightly edge Express. Both are mature but Lodash maintains better architectural hygiene.
Svelte's compiler approach and DX innovation mog Angular's complexity. Angular's enterprise baggage (HC 7.0) drags it down vs Svelte's elegance.
Zustand's simplicity mogs Redux's boilerplate. While Redux pioneered predictable state (HC 7.8), Zustand achieves similar guarantees with less ceremony.
Date-fns absolutely demolishes deprecated Moment.js. This is what PSL decay looks like - Moment's technical debt (TD 7) tanks it to 4.5 PSL.
Hono slightly reverse-mogged by Express due to lower market presence. While Hono has better scalability potential (8.0), Express's ecosystem maturity gives it a narrow edge.
Vite's build tool revolution mogs Express's server framework paradigm. Cross-category comparison, but Vite's modern DX dominates.
The most brutal mogging in the dataset. React at peak performance vs Moment in deprecated decay. This is generational extinction-level mogging.
Lodash's broader utility scope and higher market presence mog date-fns's niche focus. Both are well-designed (AS >7.5), but Lodash has wider impact.
Vitest's Vite integration and speed mog Webpack's complexity. Testing framework vs bundler, but Vitest represents the next-gen tooling wave.
Fastify's performance focus and schema validation mog Webpack's configuration nightmares. Cross-category but clear quality gap.
Vue's progressive framework philosophy mogs Angular's enterprise complexity. Vue achieves feature parity (FP 8.0) with half the cognitive load.
Svelte's no-runtime compiler mogs Webpack's bundling overhead. Both are build-time tools, and Svelte's innovation stands out.
Redux barely mogs Angular through better harmonic cohesion (7.8 vs 7.0). Both are aging but Redux's predictability gives slim advantage.
Vite (tooling) mogs Vue (framework) despite being from same ecosystem. Vite's innovation velocity outpaces Vue's incremental improvements.
React absolutely obliterates Angular. The framework wars are over - React's 2.8 PSL gap represents a decisive victory. Angular's Enterprise™ baggage weighs it down.
Zustand's simplicity mogs Webpack's complexity. Cross-category but the quality gap is undeniable.
Vitest edges Vue through superior feature prominence (8.5 vs 8.0). Same ecosystem, but Vitest represents newer innovation.
Fastify's maturity mogs Hono's emerging status. Both are performance-focused servers, but Fastify has better market presence (7.3 vs 6.5).
Lodash's active maintenance brutally mogs Moment's deprecated state. This shows what happens when technical debt catches up - complete quality collapse.
Angular barely mogs Webpack. Both are aging enterprise tools struggling to innovate. This is looksmatch territory for legacy bots.
Hono's edge-first architecture mogs Webpack's traditional bundling. Hono represents modern deployment reality.
Even date-fns, a niche utility, mogs Webpack. This illustrates how far Webpack has fallen in the modern tooling landscape.
Redux's predictable state management obliterates Moment's deprecated mess. Even Redux (not cutting-edge) easily mogs genuinely poor bots.
Svelte's compiler innovation destroys Moment's deprecated state. This is generational gap in action.
Even Webpack (itself struggling) brutally mogs Moment. When you're getting mogged by Webpack, it's truly over.

insights: React dominates with highest average μ (492) across all matchups
Moment.js receives brutal mogging (avg μ = -456) from all competitors
Webpack is the most mogged non-deprecated bot (avg μ received = -198)
Only 1/30 comparisons is a true looksmatch (Hono vs Express)
